wells.name: The Perfect Storm
wells.issue: A nightmare well with multiple, compounding failures: severe casing deformation, a hard scale blockage, and a failed primary safety valve. Requires a complex, multi-stage intervention plan.

history.problem: Unable to pass 8,500ft due to casing restriction.
history.lesson: This well combines multiple known failure modes from this field into a single asset.

history.problem: Well died after brief flow period. Pressure analysis suggests deep blockage.
history.lesson: Suspect combination of scale and integrity issues.

history.problem: TRSSV failed to close on command. Well shut-in on annulus valves.

wells.name: CASE STUDY: The Montrose Squeeze
wells.issue: SOLUTION: Casing deformation was successfully remediated with an expandable patch.

history.problem: Standard 2.313" OD toolstring unable to pass 8,500ft, encountering a hard stop.
history.lesson: Significant reservoir depletion in this area is causing geomechanical stresses leading to casing deformation, a known regional risk. MFC log confirms this is ovalization, not collapse.

history.problem: Successfully installed a 60ft expandable steel patch across the deformed section.
history.lesson: This operation proves that an expandable patch is a viable, rigless solution for restoring full-bore access in this field, restoring production and well access.

wells.name: CASE STUDY: The Scale Trap
wells.issue: SOLUTION: Severe BaSO4 scale was successfully removed with a chemical/jetting treatment.

history.problem: PLT toolstring unable to pass 9,200ft due to a hard obstruction. Produced water analysis confirmed high Barium and Sulfate content.
history.lesson: Commingling of injected seawater and formation water is causing severe, insoluble scale deposition. A previous attempt to mill scale on a nearby well resulted in stuck pipe.

history.problem: A 48hr soak with DTPA dissolver followed by a run with a high-pressure rotating jetting tool successfully cleared the blockage.
history.lesson: This two-stage approach is a proven, lower-risk method for removing hard scale compared to aggressive milling.

wells.name: CASE STUDY: The Broken Barrier
wells.issue: SOLUTION: Failed TRSSV was locked open and replaced with a slickline-retrievable insert valve.

history.problem: Valve failed to close reliably during routine 6-month test. Well was mandatorily shut-in by regulatory authority.
history.lesson: An attempted repair on a similar well with a hydraulic tool failed; a mechanical lock-open tool is more reliable.

history.problem: Successfully locked open the failed valve with a mechanical tool and installed a new wireline-retrievable insert valve.
history.lesson: This standard slickline operation is a proven, cost-effective method for restoring the primary safety barrier without a rig.

wells.name: CASE STUDY: The Sandstorm
wells.issue: SOLUTION: Failed sand screen was repaired with a through-tubing patch.

history.problem: Replaced choke for the 3rd time in 6 months due to severe erosional wear from high sand content.
history.lesson: Choking back the well is a temporary fix; the root cause of sand control failure must be addressed. Downhole video confirmed screen erosion.

history.problem: Successfully installed an expandable patch across the failed sand screen, restoring sand control.
history.lesson: This confirms that a through-tubing patch is a viable rigless repair for this failure mode in this field.

wells.name: CASE STUDY: The Wax Plug
wells.issue: SOLUTION: Severe paraffin wax blockage was cleared using CT with a chemical/mechanical approach.

history.problem: Gauge ring tagged a soft, waxy obstruction at 6,000ft. Unable to pass.
history.lesson: A previous attempt on another well with only chemicals was slow and ineffective; a combined approach is needed.

history.problem: Successfully removed wax blockage using a combination of heated chemical dissolvers and a mechanical scraper tool on Coiled Tubing.
history.lesson: The dual chemical/mechanical approach is highly effective for severe paraffin blockages.
